title: tool_intercept: ToolInterceptor::clear inconsistent with extract_all_tool_calls (in_function_calls leak)
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
kiss
## Location
src/tool_intercept.rs:111-114 (clear) vs src/tool_intercept.rs:243-272 (extract_all_tool_calls)
## Violation
```rust
pub fn clear(&mut self) {
    self.buffer.clear();
    self.in_function_calls = false;
}
```

```rust
pub fn extract_all_tool_calls(&mut self) -> (...) {
    ...
    self.buffer.clear();   // does NOT touch in_function_calls
    (all_tools, text_parts)
}
```

`extract_all_tool_calls` clears the buffer but not the `in_function_calls` flag. After extraction, `has_pending_tool_calls` still returns `true` because `self.in_function_calls` is honored at the end of that check (line 148):

```rust
self.in_function_calls
```

So a subsequent push that contains no tool calls is reported as having pending tool calls forever. The flag is also never SET by any code path inside this module (search the file), so today the bug is dormant — but the dead flag plus the inconsistent reset is a latent bug waiting for someone to introduce a `self.in_function_calls = true` line.

## Mandated refactor
Either remove the `in_function_calls` field entirely (it has no writer) or have `extract_all_tool_calls` end with `self.clear()` instead of `self.buffer.clear()`. Document the invariant in a comment.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
